Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (residents of northern or intermediate zones)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (residents of northern or intermediate zones)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends subsection 110.7(1) of the Income Tax Act to provide that Haida Gwaii is a northern zone for the purposes of section 110.7 of that Act.
Whereas residents of Haida Gwaii experience high costs of living that are due to the remote archipelagoâs isolated geography;

Whereas the northern residents deduction was established to provide tax relief to residents of northern and isolated communities, thereby offsetting the higher cost of living and accessing services in these areas;

Whereas the northern residents deduction was also intended to help employers recruit and retain workers in northern and isolated areas, such as Haida Gwaii, where employment tends to be concentrated in resource sectors that are seasonal or subject to cyclical fluctuations;

Whereas Haida Gwaii is currently a prescribed intermediate zone for the purposes of the northern residents deduction, yet prior to regulatory amendments in 1993, Haida Gwaii residents were entitled to the full deduction, as it was then a prescribed northern zone;
Whereas the remote nature of Haida Gwaii is evidenced by the fact that employees of the Government of Canada and of Crown corporations who work there receive an isolation allowance;
Whereas, compared to larger metropolitan centres, many northern and rural communities face economic hardship, lack reliable access to services and struggle to attract and retain skilled workers;

Whereas the use of latitude to determine the prescribed northern zone is inherently arbitrary and fails to consider other factors that contribute to the cost of living, such as population size, accessibility and transportation options;

Whereas the particular remoteness of Haida Gwaii communities and the high cost of living faced by their residents illustrate the need to reform the northern residents deduction to reflect their lived experience;
Now, therefore, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Haida Gwaii Residents Tax Deduction Act.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 110.7(1) of the Income Tax Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Residing in northern or intermediate zone

Subsection.Text: When, throughout a period (in this section referred to as the âqualifying periodâ) of not less than six consecutive months beginning or ending in a taxation year, a taxpayer who is an individual has resided in one or more particular areas each of which is a northern zone referred to in section 1 of Schedule 1 or an intermediate zone referred to in section 2 of that Schedule for the year and files for the year a claim in prescribed form, there may be deducted in computing the taxpayerâs taxable income for the year
Paragraphs 110.7(2)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the area is a northern zone referred to in section 1 of Schedule 1 for the year, 100%; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the area is an intermediate zone referred to in section 2 of Schedule 1 for the year, 50%.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 110.7(4)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: can reasonably be considered to be attributable to that portion of the qualifying period that is in the year and during which the taxpayer maintained a self-contained domestic establishment as the taxpayerâs principal place of residence in an area other than a northern zone referred to in section 1 of Schedule 1 or an intermediate zone referred to in section 2 of that Schedule for the year.

Subsection.Text: Section 110.7 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):
The Governor in Council may, by order, amend Schedule 1 by adding, deleting or amending the name or the coordinates of an area.

Section.Text: The reference to âscheduleâ in paragraph (g) of the definition financial institution in subsection 181(1) of the Act is replaced by a reference to âSchedule 2â.
The schedule to the Act is renumbered as Schedule 2 and is repositioned accordingly.
The Act is amended by adding, before Schedule 2, the Schedule 1 set out in the schedule to this Act.
Sections 2 to 5 apply to the 2021 and subsequent taxation years.

Schedule.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (residents of northern or intermediate zones)

Provision.Text: For the purposes of section 110.7, the northern zones are as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the Yukon Territory, the Northwest Territories or Nunavut;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: those parts of British Columbia, Alberta and Saskatchewan that lie north of 57Â°30â²N latitude;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: that part of Manitoba that lies

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: north of 56Â°20â²N latitude, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: north of 52Â°30â²N latitude and east of 95Â°25â²W longitude;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: that part of Ontario that lies

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: north of 52Â°30â²N latitude, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: north of 51Â°05â²N latitude and east of 89Â°10â²W longitude;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: that part of Quebec that lies

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: north of 51Â°05â²N latitude, or

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: north of the Gulf of St. Lawrence and east of 63Â°00â²W longitude;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: Labrador, including Belle Isle; or

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: Haida Gwaii.
For the purposes of section 110.7, the intermediate zones are Anticosti Island, the Magdalen Islands or Sable Island, or an area that is not a northern zone for the year and is:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: that part of British Columbia that lies

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: north of 55Â°35â²N latitude,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: north of 55Â°00â²N latitude and east of 122Â°00â²W longitude, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: north of 55Â°13â²N latitude and east of 123Â°16â²W longitude;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: that part of Alberta that lies north of 55Â°00â²N latitude;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: that part of Saskatchewan that lies

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: north of 55Â°00â²N latitude,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: north of 54Â°15â²N latitude and east of 107Â°00â²W longitude, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: north of 53Â°20â²N latitude and east of 103Â°00â²W longitude;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: that part of Manitoba that lies

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: north of 53Â°20â²N latitude,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: north of 52Â°10â²N latitude and east of 97Â°40â²W longitude, or

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: north of 51Â°30â²N latitude and east of 96Â°00â²W longitude;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: that part of Ontario that lies north of 50Â°35â²N latitude; or

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: that part of Quebec that lies

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: north of 50Â°35â²N latitude and west of 79Â°00â²W longitude,

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: north of 49Â°00â²N latitude, east of 79Â°00â²W longitude and west of 74Â°00â²W longitude,

Provision.Label: (iii)
Provision.Text: north of 50Â°00â²N latitude, east of 74Â°00â²W longitude and west of 70Â°00â²W longitude,

Provision.Label: (iv)
Provision.Text: north of 50Â°45â²N latitude, east of 70Â°00â²W longitude and west of 65Â°30â²W longitude, or

Provision.Label: (v)
Provision.Text: north of the Gulf of St. Lawrence, east of 65Â°30â²W longitude and west of 63Â°00â²W longitude.
